name: Nvidia Flow TOP
description: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows operating system.Hardware: This operator only works with Nvidia GPUs.
summary: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows operating system.Hardware: This operator only works with Nvidia GPUs.

parameters.name: Initialize
parameters.label: Initialize
parameters.description: Initializes the simulation.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: start - Starts the simulation playback.

parameters.name: Play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: play - The simulation only steps forward when Play = On, when off the simulation is paused.

parameters.name: Camera
parameters.label: Camera
parameters.description: camera - Specify the Camera COMP to view the simulation from. Note: Must be camera with Projection = Perspective.

parameters.name: Flow Emitters
parameters.label: Flow Emitters
parameters.description: emitters - Specify the Nvidia Flow Emitter COMPs to include in the simulation.

parameters.name: Sim Position
parameters.label: Sim Position
parameters.description:
simposition -  - The position of the simulation volume's center, in the world. The simulation cannot extend outside of the volume.

 simpositionx - simpositiony - simpositionz -

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: simpositionx - simpositiony - simpositionz -

parameters.name: Sim Size
parameters.label: Sim Size
parameters.description:
simsize -  - The size of the simulation volume in the world. The simulation cannot extend outside of the volume. Also controls the size of simulation blocks, so the total number of blocks in the volume stays the same. Smaller size blocks will require more blocks for the same size simulation. This increases accuracy but makes the simulation more taxing on the  as there are more blocks to calculate.

 simsizex - simsizey - simsizez -

parameters.name: Mem Usage
parameters.label: Mem Usage
parameters.description: memusage - Controls relative memory usage, the fraction of the total simulation blocks that will be allocated. Most simulations will not fill the simulation volume uniformly, so only a small value is needed. High values will cause extremely high memory usage.

parameters.name: Show Blocks
parameters.label: Show Blocks
parameters.description: showblocks - Displays the simulation blocks being used. Useful for debugging or optimizing your Flow simulation. Also shows the edges of the simulation volume.

parameters.name: Show Emit Bounds
parameters.label: Show Emit Bounds
parameters.description: showemitbounds - Displays the bounds of the emitters.

parameters.name: Show Shapes
parameters.label: Show Shapes
parameters.description: showshapes - Displays the shapes of the emitters.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: speed - Controls the update rate of the simulation.

parameters.name: Max Simulation Steps
parameters.label: Max Simulation Steps
parameters.description: maxsteps - Maximum number of simulation steps per update. A higher number of steps will increase quality for fast moving object at the cost of performance.

parameters.name: Render Mode
parameters.label: Render Mode
parameters.description: rendermode - Provides two debug render modes in addition to the default of Density. Debug Density gives a "rainbow" render, where density is mapped to a colour, and Dubug Velocity converts velocity xyz to an rgb colour.

parameters.name: Gravity
parameters.label: Gravity
parameters.description:
gravity -  - Gravity direction for use with Buoyancy parameter, where amount controls strength of buoyancy force.

 gravityx - gravityy - gravityz -

parameters.name: Velocity Damping
parameters.label: Velocity Damping
parameters.description: veldamping - Higher values reduce velocity faster. Uses exponential decay curve.

parameters.name: Velocity Fade
parameters.label: Velocity Fade
parameters.description: velfade - Compared to damping, fade reduces low velocity values faster. Fade velocity rate is in units per second.

parameters.name: Vel MacCormack Blend
parameters.label: Vel MacCormack Blend
parameters.description: velmaccormackblend - Higher values make a sharper appearance, but with more artifacts.

parameters.name: Smoke Damping
parameters.label: Smoke Damping
parameters.description: smokedamping - Higher values reduce smoke faster. Uses exponential decay curve.

parameters.name: Smoke Fade
parameters.label: Smoke Fade
parameters.description: smokefade - Compared to damping, fade reduces low smoke values faster. Fade velocity rate is in units per second.

parameters.name: Smoke MacCormack Blend
parameters.label: Smoke MacCormack Blend
parameters.description: smokemaccormackblend - Higher values make a sharper appearance, but with more artifacts.

parameters.name: Temp Damping
parameters.label: Temp Damping
parameters.description: tempdamping - Higher values reduce temperature faster. Uses exponential decay curve.

parameters.name: Temp Fade
parameters.label: Temp Fade
parameters.description: tempfade - Compared to damping, fade reduces low temperature values faster. Fade velocity rate is in units per second.

parameters.name: Fuel Damping
parameters.label: Fuel Damping
parameters.description: fueldamping - Higher values reduce fuel faster. Uses exponential decay curve.

parameters.name: Fuel Fade
parameters.label: Fuel Fade
parameters.description: fuelfade - Compared to damping, fade reduces low fuel values faster. Fade velocity rate is in units per second.

parameters.name: Vorticity Strength
parameters.label: Vorticity Strength
parameters.description: vortstrength - Controls amount of rotation turbulence as a multiplier, a value of 0 will result in no vorticity. High values increase turbulent flow while low values increase laminar flow.

parameters.name: Vorticity from Velocity
parameters.label: Vorticity from Velocity
parameters.description: vortfromvel - Amount of vorticity added from velocity.

parameters.name: Vorticity from Smoke
parameters.label: Vorticity from Smoke
parameters.description: vortfromsmoke - Amount of vorticity added from smoke.

parameters.name: Vorticity from Temp
parameters.label: Vorticity from Temp
parameters.description: vortfromtemp - Amount of vorticity added from temperature.

parameters.name: Vorticity from Fuel
parameters.label: Vorticity from Fuel
parameters.description: vortfromfuel - Amount of vorticity added from fuel.

parameters.name: Vorticity Constant
parameters.label: Vorticity Constant
parameters.description: vortconstant - The baseline vorticity in the simulation.

parameters.name: Ignition Temp
parameters.label: Ignition Temp
parameters.description: ignitiontemp - Specify the minimum temperature required for combustion.

parameters.name: Burn per Temp
parameters.label: Burn per Temp
parameters.description: burnpertemp - Control how much fuel is burned for a given temperature level. Lower Burn per Temp may result in some fuel not burning completely at a certain temperature.

parameters.name: Smoke per Burn
parameters.label: Smoke per Burn
parameters.description: smokeperburn - Controls amount of smoke generated for each unit of combustion (per burn).

parameters.name: Temp per Burn
parameters.label: Temp per Burn
parameters.description: tempperburn - Controls amount of temperature generated for each unit of combustion (per burn).

parameters.name: Fuel per Burn
parameters.label: Fuel per Burn
parameters.description: fuelperburn - Controls amount of fuel used for each unit of combustion (per burn).

parameters.name: Buoyancy
parameters.label: Buoyancy
parameters.description: buoyancy - Works in conjunction with the parameter Gravity above which sets a vector for use by Buoyancy. Higher values result in greater effect by the Gravity parameter.

parameters.name: Cooling Rate
parameters.label: Cooling Rate
parameters.description: coolingrate - The rate of cooling in the system, exponential.

parameters.name: Expansion
parameters.label: Expansion
parameters.description: expansion - Controls the amount the system's gaseous volume expands.

parameters.name: Velocity Alloc Weight
parameters.label: Velocity Alloc Weight
parameters.description: velallocweight - If zero, block allocation and deallocation depend on an internal threshold and weighting of Velocity. If non-zero,  block allocation and deallocation will be affected by the value of Velocity Alloc Threshold.

parameters.name: Velocity Alloc Threshold
parameters.label: Velocity Alloc Threshold
parameters.description: velallocthreshold - If Velocity Alloc Weight is non-zero, block allocation and deallocation is based on this value. In particular, if the velocity magnitude is below this threshold, the block will be deallocated, unless smoke and fuel thresholding (see below) are active.

parameters.name: Smoke Alloc Weight
parameters.label: Smoke Alloc Weight
parameters.description: smokeallocweight - If zero, block allocation and deallocation depend on an internal threshold and weighting of Smoke density. If non-zero, smoke density affects block allocation and deallocation based on the value of Smoke Alloc Threshold.

parameters.name: Smoke Alloc Threshold
parameters.label: Smoke Alloc Threshold
parameters.description: smokeallocthreshold - If Smoke Alloc Weight is non-zero, block allocation and deallocation is based on this value. In particular, if the Smoke density is below this threshold, the block will be deallocated, unless velocity and fuel thresholding are active.

parameters.name: Fuel Alloc Weight
parameters.label: Fuel Alloc Weight
parameters.description: fuelallocweight - If zero, block allocation and deallocation depend on an internal threshold and weighting of fuel density. If non-zero, fuel density affects block allocation and deallocation based on the value of Fuel Alloc Threshold.

parameters.name: Fuel Alloc Threshold
parameters.label: Fuel Alloc Threshold
parameters.description: fuelallocthreshold - If Fuel Alloc Weight is non-zero, block allocation and deallocation is based on this value. In particular, if the Smoke density is below this threshold, the block will be deallocated, unless velocity and smoke thresholding are active.

parameters.name: Enable
parameters.label: Enable
parameters.description: enableshadow - Enables Volume shadow rendering for the simulation. Shadowing generates light intensity values that overwrite the "burn" channel of the grid. Values range from 0 to 1, where 0 is fully shadowed. Shadow visibilty can be controlled by enabling 'Override Emitter Intensity Mask' or by adjusting the 'Burn Intensity Mask', 'Burn Alpha Mask', and 'Burn Color Mask' in Nvidia Flow Emitter COMP

parameters.name: Draw Debug
parameters.label: Draw Debug
parameters.description: drawshadowdebug - Displays the volume shadow blocks being used. Useful for debugging or optimizing.

parameters.name: Override Emitter Intensity Mask
parameters.label: Override Emitter Intensity Mask
parameters.description: overrideemitter - Automatically adjusts the emitters' render material, so volume shadows are visible. Will override 'Burn Intensity Mask' and 'Intensity Bias' of emitters.

parameters.name: Light
parameters.label: Light
parameters.description: light - The light source used to generate volume shadow. The light should be a shadow caster. Only the position and orientation of the light are used, color and intensity are ignored.

parameters.name: Shadow Resolution
parameters.label: Shadow Resolution
parameters.description: shadowresolution - The resolution of the shadow map volume texture. The texture is allocated as a cube, so there will be space for shadowresolution^3 blocks.

parameters.name: Min Memory Usage
parameters.label: Min Memory Usage
parameters.description: shadowminusage - The initial fraction of volume shadow blocks to allocate memory for.

parameters.name: Max Memory Usage
parameters.label: Max Memory Usage
parameters.description: shadowmaxusage - The maximum fraction of volume shadow blocks to allocate memory for.

parameters.name: Intensity Scale
parameters.label: Intensity Scale
parameters.description: shadowintensityscale - Scales how dark the shadow will be.

parameters.name: Min Intensity
parameters.label: Min Intensity
parameters.description: shadowminintensity - A lower limit for shadow intensity.

parameters.name: Burn Blend Mask
parameters.label: Burn Blend Mask
parameters.description: shadowburnmask - Allows the burn value in the simulation to control the blend strength of the shadow. Postive values mean burn increases the blend strength, negative values mean burn decreases the blend strength

parameters.name: Smoke Blend Mask
parameters.label: Smoke Blend Mask
parameters.description: shadowsmokemask - Allows the smoke value in the simulation to control the blend strength of the shadow. Postive values mean smoke increases the blend strength, negative values mean smoke decreases the blend strength

parameters.name: Temp Blend Mask
parameters.label: Temp Blend Mask
parameters.description: shadowtempmask - Allows the temperature value in the simulation to control the blend strength of the shadow. Postive values mean temp increases the blend strength, negative values mean temp decreases the blend strength

parameters.name: Fuel Blend Mask
parameters.label: Fuel Blend Mask
parameters.description: shadowfuelmask - Allows the fuel value in the simulation to control the blend strength of the shadow. Postive values mean fuel increases the blend strength, negative values mean fuel decreases the blend strength

parameters.name: Blend Bias
parameters.label: Blend Bias
parameters.description: shadowblendbias - An offset that increases or decreases the blend strength by a constant amount. Parts of the grid with a blend value over 1 will have shadows. Parts of the grid with blend value under 1 will not have shadows. Blend values between 0 and 1 scale the strength of shadow.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail. When the input is 32-bit float format, only nearest filtering will be used (regardless of what is selected).

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. For every pass after the first it takes the result of the previous pass and replaces the node's first input with the result of the previous pass. One exception to this is the GLSL TOP when using compute shaders, where the input will continue to be the connected 's image.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
